features.name: Improved Critical
features.text: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.text: Add half your proficiency bonus to Str, Dex, or Con checks that don't already use it.

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.text: Halve the time and gold to copy evocation spells into your spellbook.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.text: When you cast an evocation spell affecting others, you can protect allies from its effects.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.text: You always have these domain spells prepared, and they don't count against your number of prepared spells: 1st—bless, cure wounds; 3rd—lesser restoration, spiritual weapon; 5th—beacon of hope, revivify; 7th—death ward, guardian of faith; 9th—mass cure wounds, raise dead.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.text: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

features.name: Disciple of Life
features.text: Whenever a spell of 1st level or higher restores hit points to a creature, it regains extra hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Preserve Life
features.text: As an action, use Channel Divinity to restore hit points equal to five times your cleric level, divided among creatures within 30 feet (up to half each one's maximum).

features.name: Blessed Healer
features.text: When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher that restores hit points to another creature, you regain hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.text: Once per turn, when you hit with a weapon attack, deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage (2d8 at 14th level).

features.name: Supreme Healing
features.text: When you would roll dice to restore hit points with a spell, use the highest possible value for each die instead.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.text: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.text: When a creature you can see within 60 feet makes an attack, ability check, or damage roll, use your reaction and a Bardic Inspiration die to subtract the roll.

features.name: Additional Magical Secrets
features.text: Learn two spells from any class's spell list; they don't count against your number of bard spells known.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.text: When you make an ability check, you can expend a Bardic Inspiration die and add it to the roll.

features.name: Bonus Cantrip
features.text: You learn one additional druid cantrip of your choice.

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.text: Once per day during a short rest, recover expended spell slots totaling up to half your druid level (rounded up), none higher than 5th level.

features.name: Circle Spells
features.text: Choose a land type (arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, swamp, or Underdark). It grants always-prepared bonus spells at 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level that don't count against your prepared total.

features.name: Land's Stride
features.text: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs no extra movement, and you pass through plants without being slowed or harmed; advantage on saves against plants magically created to impede movement.

features.name: Nature's Ward
features.text: You can't be charmed or frightened by elementals or fey, and you're immune to poison and disease.

features.name: Nature's Sanctuary
features.text: Beasts and plants must succeed on a Wisdom save to attack you, or choose a new target.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.text: When you hit with a Flurry of Blows attack, you can also knock the target prone, push it 15 feet, or prevent it from taking reactions (Strength or Dexterity save).

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.text: As an action, regain hit points equal to three times your monk level, once per long rest.

features.name: Tranquility
features.text: At the end of a long rest you gain the effect of a sanctuary spell until your next long rest.

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.text: When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, spend 3 ki to set up lethal vibrations you can later end as an action (Constitution save or drop to 0 hit points).

features.name: Oath Spells
features.text: You always have these oath spells prepared (they don't count against your total): 3rd—protection from evil and good, sanctuary; 5th—lesser restoration, zone of truth; 9th—beacon of hope, dispel magic; 13th—freedom of movement, guardian of faith; 17th—commune, flame strike.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Sacred Weapon
features.text: As an action, imbue a weapon with positive energy for 1 minute: add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls with it, and it emits bright light.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Turn the Unholy
features.text: As an action, present your holy symbol; each fiend or undead within 30 feet that fails a Wisdom save is turned for 1 minute.

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.text: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet can't be charmed while you're conscious.

features.name: Purity of Spirit
features.text: You're always under the effect of a protection from evil and good spell.

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.text: As an action, emanate sunlight for 1 minute: enemies starting their turn within 30 feet take radiant damage, and you have advantage on saves against spells cast by fiends and undead.

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.text: Choose Colossus Slayer (extra 1d8 to a damaged target once per turn), Giant Killer (reaction attack vs Large+ foes), or Horde Breaker (a second attack on a different nearby target).

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.text: Choose Escape the Horde, Multiattack Defense, or Steel Will.

features.name: Multiattack
features.text: Choose Volley (ranged attack against all foes in a 10-foot radius) or Whirlwind Attack (melee attack against all foes within 5 feet).

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.text: Choose Evasion, Stand Against the Tide, or Uncanny Dodge.

features.name: Fast Hands
features.text: Use your Cunning Action bonus action to make a Sleight of Hand check, use thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or use an object.

features.name: Second-Story Work
features.text: Climbing no longer costs extra movement, and your running jump distance increases by your Dexterity modifier (in feet).

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.text: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn.

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.text: You ignore all class, race, and level requirements on the use of magic items.

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.text: You can take two turns during the first round of any combat (the second at initiative minus 10).
